posting.sourceTitle: International Union of Bricklayers & Allied Craftsmen Local 3 NY (Ithaca Chapter)
posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship Committee for the International Union of Bricklayers & Allied Craftsmen (IUBAC), Local Union #3
posting.applicationSummary: Obtain and submit applications online at www.bac3ny.com or in person at the JAC Training Center in Whitney Point during the recruitment period.
posting.applicationInPersonAddress: JAC for the IUBAC, Local Union #3 Training Center, 4 Railroad Street, Whitney Point, NY 13862
posting.applicationHours: Mondays only, 9:00 a.m.-12:00 p.m. and 1:00 p.m.-3:00 p.m., excluding legal holidays, during the recruitment period
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or high school equivalency diploma such as GED or TASC.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.
posting.classRequirement: Must be able to attend required classes at the approved school.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old.
Must have a high school diploma or high school equivalency diploma (such as TASC or GED).

Must sign an affidavit stating that they are physically able to perform the work of these trades, including working with sharp objects and hazardous machinery, possible exposure to noise and respiratory irritants, prolonged standing and walking, repeated squatting and/or bending, working indoors and outdoors in all kinds of weather, working in confined spaces, lifting and moving heavy objects and equipment up to 50 pounds, and working on ladders and scaffolds exceeding 50 feet.
Must have reliable means of transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school.
Must be prepared to take a math and measurement computation test at the time of interview.
Must provide DD-214, Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty, if applicable, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 18, have a high school diploma or equivalency, have reliable transportation, and be prepared for a math and measurement test and a physical-ability affidavit.
posting.contactName: JAC for the IUBAC, Local Union #3
posting.contactSummary: Contact the JAC for the IUBAC, Local Union #3 at (607) 786-8945 for further information.

oes.socDescription: Apply hard tile, marble, and wood tile to walls, floors, ceilings, and roof decks.

onet.title: Tile and Stone Setters
onet.description: Apply hard tile, stone, and comparable materials to walls, floors, ceilings, countertops, and roof decks.

task.title: Align and straighten tile using levels, squares, and straightedges.
Finish and dress the joints and wipe excess grout from between tiles, using damp sponge.
Cut and shape tile to fit around obstacles and into odd spaces and corners, using hand and power cutting tools.
Determine and implement the best layout to achieve a desired pattern.
Mix, apply, and spread plaster, concrete, mortar, cement, mastic, glue or other adhesives to form a bed for the tiles, using brush, trowel and screed.
Study blueprints and examine surface to be covered to determine amount of material needed.
Measure and mark surfaces to be tiled, following blueprints.
Lay and set mosaic tiles to create decorative wall, mural, and floor designs.
Apply mortar to tile back, position the tile, and press or tap with trowel handle to affix tile to base.
Mix and apply mortar or cement to edges and ends of drain tiles to seal halves and joints.
Apply a sealer to make grout stain- and water-resistant.
Level concrete and allow to dry.
Measure and cut metal lath to size for walls and ceilings, using tin snips.
Install and anchor fixtures in designated positions, using hand tools.
Prepare surfaces for tiling by attaching lath or waterproof paper, or by applying a cement mortar coat to a metal screen.
Remove and replace cracked or damaged tile.
Cut tile backing to required size, using shears.
Remove any old tile, grout and adhesive using chisels and scrapers and clean the surface carefully.
Cut, surface, polish, and install marble and granite or install pre-cast terrazzo, granite or marble units.
Spread mastic or other adhesive base on roof deck to form base for promenade tile, using serrated spreader.
Assist customers in selection of tile and grout.
Prepare cost and labor estimates, based on calculations of time and materials needed for project.
Brush glue onto manila paper on which design has been drawn and position tiles, finished side down, onto paper.
Select and order tile and other items to be installed, such as bathroom accessories, walls, panels, and cabinets, according to specifications.
Build underbeds and install anchor bolts, wires, and brackets.

activity.title: Align masonry materials.
Install masonry materials.
Remove excess materials from finished construction projects.
Apply mortar.
Cut tile, stone, or other masonry materials.
Mix substances or compounds needed for work activities.
Spread concrete or other aggregate mixtures.
Determine construction project layouts.
Estimate materials requirements for projects.
Review blueprints or specifications to determine work requirements.
Mark reference points on construction materials.
Measure work site dimensions.
Apply adhesives to construction materials.
Apply sealants or other protective coatings.
Cut metal components for installation.
Measure materials or objects for installation or assembly.
Install building fixtures.
Remove worn, damaged or outdated materials from work areas.
Communicate with clients about products, procedures, and policies.
Estimate construction project costs.
Estimate construction project labor requirements.
Clean surfaces in preparation for work activities.
Cut carpet, vinyl or other flexible materials.
Smooth surfaces with abrasive materials or tools.
Order construction or extraction materials or equipment.
Select construction materials.

element.name: Active Listening
element.description: Giving full attention to what other people are saying, taking time to understand the points being made, asking questions as appropriate, and not interrupting at inappropriate times.

element.name: Complex Problem Solving
element.description: Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions.

element.name: Coordination
element.description: Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions.

element.name: Critical Thinking
element.description: Using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions, or approaches to problems.

element.name: Mathematics
element.description: Using mathematics to solve problems.

element.name: Speaking
element.description: Talking to others to convey information effectively.

element.name: Time Management
element.description: Managing one's own time and the time of others.

element.name: Reading Comprehension
element.description: Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work-related documents.

element.name: Judgment and Decision Making
element.description: Considering the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one.

element.name: Monitoring
element.description: Monitoring/Assessing performance of yourself, other individuals, or organizations to make improvements or take corrective action.

element.name: Instructing
element.description: Teaching others how to do something.

element.name: Quality Control Analysis
element.description: Conducting tests and inspections of products, services, or processes to evaluate quality or performance.

element.name: Service Orientation
element.description: Actively looking for ways to help people.

element.name: Operations Monitoring
element.description: Watching gauges, dials, or other indicators to make sure a machine is working properly.

element.name: Social Perceptiveness
element.description: Being aware of others' reactions and understanding why they react as they do.

element.name: Active Learning
element.description: Understanding the implications of new information for both current and future problem-solving and decision-making.

element.name: Negotiation
element.description: Bringing others together and trying to reconcile differences.

element.name: Management of Personnel Resources
element.description: Motivating, developing, and directing people as they work, identifying the best people for the job.

element.name: Operation and Control
element.description: Controlling operations of equipment or systems.

element.name: Persuasion
element.description: Persuading others to change their minds or behavior.

element.name: Equipment Selection
element.description: Determining the kind of tools and equipment needed to do a job.

element.name: Learning Strategies
element.description: Selecting and using training/instructional methods and procedures appropriate for the situation when learning or teaching new things.

element.name: Systems Analysis
element.description: Determining how a system should work and how changes in conditions, operations, and the environment will affect outcomes.

element.name: Equipment Maintenance
element.description: Performing routine maintenance on equipment and determining when and what kind of maintenance is needed.

element.name: Management of Material Resources
element.description: Obtaining and seeing to the appropriate use of equipment, facilities, and materials needed to do certain work.

element.name: Repairing
element.description: Repairing machines or systems using the needed tools.

element.name: Systems Evaluation
element.description: Identifying measures or indicators of system performance and the actions needed to improve or correct performance, relative to the goals of the system.

element.name: Troubleshooting
element.description: Determining causes of operating errors and deciding what to do about it.

element.name: Writing
element.description: Communicating effectively in writing as appropriate for the needs of the audience.

element.name: Operations Analysis
element.description: Analyzing needs and product requirements to create a design.

element.name: Technology Design
element.description: Generating or adapting equipment and technology to serve user needs.

element.name: Installation
element.description: Installing equipment, machines, wiring, or programs to meet specifications.

element.name: Management of Financial Resources
element.description: Determining how money will be spent to get the work done, and accounting for these expenditures.

element.name: Science
element.description: Using scientific rules and methods to solve problems.

element.name: Programming
element.description: Writing computer programs for various purposes.

element.name: Visualization
element.description: The ability to imagine how something will look after it is moved around or when its parts are moved or rearranged.

element.name: Near Vision
element.description: The ability to see details at close range (within a few feet of the observer).

element.name: Extent Flexibility
element.description: The ability to bend, stretch, twist, or reach with your body, arms, and/or legs.

element.name: Problem Sensitivity
element.description: The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing that there is a problem.

element.name: Trunk Strength
element.description: The ability to use your abdominal and lower back muscles to support part of the body repeatedly or continuously over time without "giving out" or fatiguing.

element.name: Information Ordering
element.description: The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules (e.g., patterns of numbers, letters, words, pictures, mathematical operations).

element.name: Arm-Hand Steadiness
element.description: The ability to keep your hand and arm steady while moving your arm or while holding your arm and hand in one position.

element.name: Category Flexibility
element.description: The ability to generate or use different sets of rules for combining or grouping things in different ways.

element.name: Far Vision
element.description: The ability to see details at a distance.

element.name: Finger Dexterity
element.description: The ability to make precisely coordinated movements of the fingers of one or both hands to grasp, manipulate, or assemble very small objects.

element.name: Manual Dexterity
element.description: The ability to quickly move your hand, your hand together with your arm, or your two hands to grasp, manipulate, or assemble objects.

element.name: Multilimb Coordination
element.description: The ability to coordinate two or more limbs (for example, two arms, two legs, or one leg and one arm) while sitting, standing, or lying down. It does not involve performing the activities while the whole body is in motion.

element.name: Oral Comprehension
element.description: The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.

element.name: Stamina
element.description: The ability to exert yourself physically over long periods of time without getting winded or out of breath.

element.name: Static Strength
element.description: The ability to exert maximum muscle force to lift, push, pull, or carry objects.

element.name: Control Precision
element.description: The ability to quickly and repeatedly adjust the controls of a machine or a vehicle to exact positions.

element.name: Deductive Reasoning
element.description: The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense.

element.name: Inductive Reasoning
element.description: The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events).

element.name: Oral Expression
element.description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.

element.name: Speech Clarity
element.description: The ability to speak clearly so others can understand you.

element.name: Speech Recognition
element.description: The ability to identify and understand the speech of another person.

element.name: Visual Color Discrimination
element.description: The ability to match or detect differences between colors, including shades of color and brightness.

element.name: Gross Body Equilibrium
element.description: The ability to keep or regain your body balance or stay upright when in an unstable position.

element.name: Selective Attention
element.description: The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted.

element.name: Written Comprehension
element.description: The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing.

element.name: Gross Body Coordination
element.description: The ability to coordinate the movement of your arms, legs, and torso together when the whole body is in motion.

element.name: Mathematical Reasoning
element.description: The ability to choose the right mathematical methods or formulas to solve a problem.

element.name: Originality
element.description: The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem.

element.name: Perceptual Speed
element.description: The ability to quickly and accurately compare similarities and differences among sets of letters, numbers, objects, pictures, or patterns. The things to be compared may be presented at the same time or one after the other. This ability also includes comparing a presented object with a remembered object.

element.name: Depth Perception
element.description: The ability to judge which of several objects is closer or farther away from you, or to judge the distance between you and an object.

element.name: Fluency of Ideas
element.description: The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity).

element.name: Number Facility
element.description: The ability to add, subtract, multiply, or divide quickly and correctly.

element.name: Dynamic Strength
element.description: The ability to exert muscle force repeatedly or continuously over time. This involves muscular endurance and resistance to muscle fatigue.

element.name: Flexibility of Closure
element.description: The ability to identify or detect a known pattern (a figure, object, word, or sound) that is hidden in other distracting material.

element.name: Reaction Time
element.description: The ability to quickly respond (with the hand, finger, or foot) to a signal (sound, light, picture) when it appears.

element.name: Response Orientation
element.description: The ability to choose quickly between two or more movements in response to two or more different signals (lights, sounds, pictures). It includes the speed with which the correct response is started with the hand, foot, or other body part.

element.name: Written Expression
element.description: The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand.

element.name: Spatial Orientation
element.description: The ability to know your location in relation to the environment or to know where other objects are in relation to you.

element.name: Speed of Closure
element.description: The ability to quickly make sense of, combine, and organize information into meaningful patterns.

element.name: Auditory Attention
element.description: The ability to focus on a single source of sound in the presence of other distracting sounds.

element.name: Memorization
element.description: The ability to remember information such as words, numbers, pictures, and procedures.

element.name: Time Sharing
element.description: The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information (such as speech, sounds, touch, or other sources).

element.name: Wrist-Finger Speed
element.description: The ability to make fast, simple, repeated movements of the fingers, hands, and wrists.

element.name: Hearing Sensitivity
element.description: The ability to detect or tell the differences between sounds that vary in pitch and loudness.

element.name: Speed of Limb Movement
element.description: The ability to quickly move the arms and legs.

element.name: Glare Sensitivity
element.description: The ability to see objects in the presence of a glare or bright lighting.

element.name: Rate Control
element.description: The ability to time your movements or the movement of a piece of equipment in anticipation of changes in the speed and/or direction of a moving object or scene.

element.name: Peripheral Vision
element.description: The ability to see objects or movement of objects to one's side when the eyes are looking ahead.

element.name: Sound Localization
element.description: The ability to tell the direction from which a sound originated.

element.name: Night Vision
element.description: The ability to see under low-light conditions.

element.name: Explosive Strength
element.description: The ability to use short bursts of muscle force to propel oneself (as in jumping or sprinting), or to throw an object.

element.name: Dynamic Flexibility
element.description: The ability to quickly and repeatedly bend, stretch, twist, or reach out with your body, arms, and/or legs.

element.name: Attention to Detail
element.description: A tendency to be detail-oriented, organized, and thorough in completing work.

element.name: Dependability
element.description: A tendency to be reliable, responsible, and consistent in meeting work-related obligations.

element.name: Cautiousness
element.description: A tendency to be careful, deliberate, and risk-avoidant when making work-related decisions or doing work.

element.name: Perseverance
element.description: A tendency to exhibit determination and resolve to perform or complete tasks in the face of difficult circumstances or obstacles at work.

element.name: Achievement Orientation
element.description: A tendency to establish and maintain personally challenging work-related goals, set high work-related standards, and exert high effort toward meeting those goals and standards.

element.name: Stress Tolerance
element.description: A tendency to cope and function effectively in stressful situations at work.

element.name: Integrity
element.description: A tendency to be honest and ethical at work.

element.name: Self-Control
element.description: A tendency to remain calm and composed and to manage emotions effectively in response to criticism or difficult situations at work.

element.name: Self-Confidence
element.description: A tendency to believe in one's work-related capabilities and ability to control one's work-related outcomes.

element.name: Adaptability
element.description: A tendency to be open to and comfortable with change, new experiences, or ideas at work.

element.name: Cooperation
element.description: A tendency to be pleasant, helpful, and willing to assist others at work.

element.name: Initiative
element.description: A tendency to be proactive and take on extra responsibilities and tasks that may fall outside of one's required work role.

element.name: Innovation
element.description: A tendency to be inventive, to be imaginative, and to adopt new perspectives on ways to accomplish work.

element.name: Sincerity
element.description: A tendency to be genuine and sincere in interactions with others at work, without concern for personal gain or self-interest.

element.name: Optimism
element.description: A tendency to exhibit a positive attitude and positive emotions at work, even under difficult circumstances.
